sc.d: Inherited a struggling AM station. Ratings soft, morning host just quit. Nowhere to go but up. You have enough runway to turn it around — if you move fast.

news.t: 📈 Fall ad market: peak season. Revenue runs ~12% above annual average. Political buys boosting News/Talk and Sports.
📡 📻 WGCG goes dark — Contemporary Hit on AM, no viable audience remaining.: undefined
📡 📻 WDPU goes dark — Adult Contemporary on AM, no viable audience remaining.: undefined
📡 Economy Recovers Strong: Clinton boom begins. Ad spending surges.
📊 WSTJ repositions toward Soft AC — management shakeup.
📊 WSIE repositions toward Traditional Country — management shakeup.
📋 Tanya Hall's contract at WFNZ expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
📋 Sandra Williams's contract at WDXE-FM expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
📋 Jerry Johnson's contract at WXJU expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
📋 Earl Thomas's contract at WFL expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
WSBO marketing budget set to $61K/period — takes effect next period.
WSBO programming budget set to $98K/period.
📉 Spring ad market: lean season. Revenue runs ~12% below annual average. Plan cash flow accordingly.
📡 📻 WFGM pledge drive — listener fatigue creates a 2-period ratings window for commercial stations.: undefined
📡 📻 WCTZ pledge drive — listener fatigue creates a 2-period ratings window for commercial stations.: undefined
📡 📡 Sports Talk Explodes: YOUR WFNZ (Sports Talk) — surge from drift positioning: undefined
📡 📡 Standards Finds Its Niche: YOUR WXJU (Adult Standards) — surge from drift positioning: undefined
📡 FCC Expands Further: FCC allows 3 AM + 3 FM per market.
⚡ WSIE poaches Frank Thompson from WTXJ
⚡ WTXJ poaches Frank Thompson from WFL
📻 WDPU abandons Urban Contemporary → relaunches as Adult Contemporary.
📊 WSTJ repositions toward Soft AC — management shakeup.
📻 WGCG abandons Oldies → relaunches as Contemporary Hit.
⚠ Ray Brown quits WFL AFTERNOON — morale collapse. Find a replacement fast.
📋 FCC: 3 AM + 3 FM
WSBO demo targeting: ADULTS 25–54
WSBO repositions: → Crossover Pop Country (25→75)
Jerry Johnson moves to WXJU MORNING
You hire Maria Thomas for WFL EVENING
You hire Earl Thomas for WFL MIDDAY
You fire Mike Young from WFL MIDDAY. Saving $40K/period going forward.
You fire Dave Walker from WFL EVENING. Saving $69K/period going forward.
You fire Ted Thomas from WFL O/NIGHT. Saving $13K/period going forward.
Frank Thompson ↔ Dave Walker swap at WFL
WFL spot load set to 12 min/hr — revenue impact next period.
📈 Fall ad market: peak season. Revenue runs ~12% above annual average.
📡 📻 WKIP goes dark — Urban Contemporary on AM, no viable audience remaining.: undefined
📡 Rush Limbaugh Effect: Talk radio audience explodes. News/Talk and Sports Talk benefit.
📡 AM Music Collapses: The last holdout music AMs reformat or go dark. FM is the only viable music signal.
⚡ WSIE poaches Steve Hall from WNRJ
📊 WFNZ repositions toward Local Teams First — management shakeup.
📋 Duke Martin's contract at WDXE-FM expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
📋 Tanya Hall's contract at WDXE-FM expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
📋 Ted Thomas's contract at WFL expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
📋 Frank Thompson's contract at WFL expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
📋 Mike Young's contract at WFL expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
📋 Dave Walker's contract at WFL expires next period — click their name to negotiate.
Don Clark moves to WSBO MORNING
You acquire WSBO (Country)  for $5900K. Expect ~22% listener churn as the station transitions — keep format and talent stable to minimize losses.
WFL marketing budget set to $68K/period — takes effect next period.

evq.d: The last Beautiful Music stations have converted. Adult Contemporary is the successor format.
Middle of Road no longer viable as a commercial format. The format is dead.
Ownership caps lifted. Up to 8 stations per market. Consolidation begins.
97% of new cars have FM. A music AM in 1997 is a museum piece.
Clear Channel and Infinity buying everything. Independents pressured.
Clear Channel and Chancellor are buying stations faster than regulators can process the filings. Independent operators are getting offers they can't refuse. The era of local radio is ending.
Silicon Valley start-ups are buying radio time. The dot-com ad boom is starting to flood local markets. Revenue is climbing ahead of expectations.
Tech companies flooding radio with ad dollars. Revenue peaks.
Rhythmic CHR and Hot AC become dominant with younger demos.
Tech ad spending collapses. Radio revenue drops sharply.
News/Talk ratings spike dramatically.
Corporate groups roll out pre-recorded DJ tracks coast to coast. One personality now voices six markets. The live local DJ is becoming an endangered species.
Post-9/11 patriotism drives Country radio to record ratings. Toby Keith is everywhere. If you're in Country, this is your moment.
Another round of News/Talk ratings boost.
XM and Sirius pull premium listeners. Quiet drag begins.
Stations adding 70s & 80s, dropping 'Oldies' branding. The music is getting younger.
Pandora launches. Terrestrial revenue begins long decline.
Mid-decade boom. Ad spending growing.
The industry spent $500 million promoting HD Radio as the answer to satellite and streaming. Nobody bought the receivers. The last technical fix for terrestrial radio has failed.

Podcasts, streaming news apps, and satellite radio are eating into AM audiences. Pure AM Talk stations without FM simulcasts will face gradual share erosion through the 2010s. An FM simulcast or translator stops this immediately.
Satellite consolidates. Drag on terrestrial intensifies.
Worst recession since 1930s. Ad spending collapses.
Green shoots. Ad market begins cautious recovery.
iHeartRadio launches. Terrestrial content finds new distribution.
Pandora's IPO values the streaming service at $2.6 billion. For the first time, Wall Street is betting against terrestrial radio. The existential threat is now priced in.
AM Talk has become the loudest voice in American politics. Ratings are strong but the demo is aging and getting narrower. You're winning the argument and losing the audience.
Podcast listening goes mainstream. Hybrid talk format unlocked.
The last pure Oldies stations have converted. Classic Hits is the successor — or the demo is 75+ and the format is dying.
Spotify growing fast. 18–34 demo drifting from terrestrial FM.
Digital ad spend growing. Radio holds share in local markets.

Clear Channel (now iHeart) and CBS Radio scramble to add FM simulcasts for their AM flagships. The AM signal quality gap vs. FM and streaming is now undeniable — even for dominant News/Talk and Sports stations.
Spotify crosses 40 million active users. The 18-34 demo now has a credible alternative to FM for music. Radio still wins in the car — for now.
Broadcast radio will generate $17.4 billion this year — the last time the industry posts growth before a multi-year slide. If you're not positioned now, you won't be.
Half of all 18–34s now stream more than they listen to radio.
Election year. News/Talk and local stations flooded with political spend.
Amazon Echo and Google Home put a radio in every kitchen — but also Spotify, podcasts, and Pandora. Radio's smart speaker presence is strong. The competition is stronger.
iHeartMedia warns of possible bankruptcy — $20 billion in debt from the Clear Channel LBO. The consolidation era is eating itself. Highly-leveraged group owners are cutting costs everywhere.
Podcast ad revenue explodes. Podcast/Talk CPM rises further.
Streaming has fundamentally reshaped radio economics.
Pandemic. Ad spending collapses overnight. Drive-time audiences vanish.
50 years in Atlanta radio. The campaign is complete.
